uses: large charm granting +3 class skills, stats, and resists
demand_drivers: different classes demand different torches — Sorceress, Paladin torches most expensive
supply_notes: dropped from Uber Tristram (requires keys + organs)
supply depends on key farming and Uber Tristram runner activity
class random — supply of specific class torches varies

common_quote_units: Jah Rune or Ber Rune (high-roll meta-class torches)
important_rolls_or_variants: class matters: Sorceress > Paladin > Barbarian > Amazon > Assassin > Necromancer > Druid
segment_sensitivity: ladder vs non-ladder: significant price and supply differences
class popularity varies by season meta
known_model_risks: price varies enormously by class and roll quality
raw VWAP across all classes and rolls is misleading
Sorceress and Paladin torches trade at multiples of Druid/Necromancer torches
class information may be in Traderie listing data but roll data may not
per-class-per-roll modeling needed for useful pricing

traderie.notes: Needs evaluation: does Traderie expose class and roll data? Per-class pricing may be feasible even without per-roll.

reddit.notes: Use for demand, sentiment, confusion points, source discovery, and item-profile updates.

d2jsp.notes: FG prices often listed per-class and per-roll range on d2jsp.

rmt_sites.notes: External cash-market signal only. Do not present as a recommendation to buy or sell.

research_notes.note: Initial draft profile. Like Annihilus, Hellfire Torch has severe roll and class variance. Per-class pricing may be feasible as a first step.

open_questions: Does the Traderie API expose class for Torch listings?
Does it expose roll data (attributes, resists)?
Is per-class VWAP useful even without per-roll refinement?
What is the current class price ranking for the active ladder?
